subject: [PATCH v4 1/3] cxl/core/region: move pmem region driver logic into region_pmem.c

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary: Raised concerns about device_add rollback in cxl/, questioning whether it's intentional or a bug. Suggested that the 'hanging objects on failure' issue is common throughout cxl/.

reviews.author: Jonathan Cameron
reviews.summary: Defended the patch's logic, stating that device_add rollback is not consistent throughout cxl/ and may be a bug even if it's unlikely to happen.
reviews.raw_body:
On Mon, 23 Mar 2026 10:47:51 -0500
Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net> wrote:

> On Mon, Mar 23, 2026 at 03:08:47PM +0000, Jonathan Cameron wrote:
> > On Sun, 22 Mar 2026 09:16:36 -0400
> > Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net> wrote:
> >   
> > > +	scoped_guard(device, &cxl_nvb->dev) {
> > > +		if (cxl_nvb->dev.driver)
> > > +			rc = devm_add_action_or_reset(&cxl_nvb->dev,
> > > +					cxlr_pmem_unregister,
> > > +					cxlr_pmem);
> > > +		else
> > > +			rc = -ENXIO;  
> > As an example. If we happen to take this path... Where is the device_add() undone?  
> 
> In many other parts of the code, device_add is not rolled back either
> "for reasons", and - depending on who you talk to - this is
> intentional... for debugging?
> 
> I dunno, but the "hanging objects on failure" thing is common throughout
> cxl/ - :shrug:

If it were consistent maybe, but in other paths in this function it is cleaned up...
So I call bug even if it's one that is relatively unlikely to actually happen.

J
> 
> ~Gregory

2026-03-23.patch_summary: The patch moves pmem region driver logic into a new file, but one reviewer questions the consistency of device_add rollback in cxl/.
